description:
Goal: remove the temporary clause-head matching and guard scaffolding left behind in Compiler2 lowered bodies once shared entry-dispatch facts are authoritative.

Change:
- Delete any `LoweredBody` fields, helpers, and tests whose only job is to reproduce function-entry clause selection or guard gating after `EntryDispatch(function)` exists.
- Collapse semantic/body consumers onto the shared dispatch facts as the sole source of clause-entry truth.
- Remove temporary affordances added during `fz-rh2.6.7` / `fz-rh2.6.10` that are no longer needed once entry dispatch is authoritative.

Why:
- Keeping both shared dispatch facts and body-local clause rematching would be a correctness and maintenance trap.
- This ticket is the explicit price of any temporary body scaffolding we tolerated while landing the new boundary.

Checks:
- Compiler2 bodies retain only local execution structure, not function-entry dispatch duplication.
- Existing dispatch/semantic tests still pass with the duplicate clause-head machinery removed.
- No dead helper code, no dual source of truth, no leftover transitional tests.

Non-goals:
- No new semantic features.
- No “temporary forever” compatibility layer.
title: Compiler2 cleanup: remove clause-head matching scaffolding from lowered bodies
